_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an index averaging the prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice and benchmarked to 2015 at 100, currently stands at 132, indicating a 32% increase since 2015. This rise reflects primarily recent and uneven price changes among the basket's components rather than a steady climb across all commodities. Some items have experienced significant price shifts while others have seen minor adjustments. The index captures global commodity prices sourced from FRED and IMF databases.
notes.movers: Coffee has seen the largest increase at 93% since 2015, followed by orange juice at 22%, wheat at 7%, and sugar at 5%. These price moves are primarily driven by specific weather events and crop diseases, such as frost and drought in arabica coffee-producing regions, and citrus greening and hurricanes affecting orange juice crops. These soft commodities are traded in relatively thin markets, meaning small supply disruptions can lead to outsized price swings. As a result, the price changes reflect localized agricultural challenges rather than broad-based inflation across commodities.
notes.why: The headline increase in the breakfast basket is largely due to a few weather-sensitive crops that are vulnerable to environmental factors and disease. Because these inputs are affected by agricultural conditions that can persist or recur, their prices may remain elevated over extended periods. This dynamic underscores the volatility and uncertainty inherent in soft commodity markets.
